member.name: Jade Botterill
member.role: MP for Ossett and Denby Dale
member.synopsis: Jade Botterill is the Labour MP for Ossett and Denby Dale , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently holds the Government post of Government Whip, Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of heavy goods vehicles travelling through villages on (a) traffic and (b) pollution.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I recognise that heavy goods vehicles (HGV) can have negative safety and environmental impact on villages, particularly where roads and pavements are narrow. Local traffic authorities have powers under Sections 1 & 2 of the Road Traffic Regulation Act...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to mitigate the impact of heavy goods vehicles travelling through villages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local traffic authorities have powers under Sections 1 & 2 of the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984, which allow for the restriction or prohibition of the use of HGVs on any road, for a wide range of safety and environmental reasons. These measures can...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has made an assessment of the potential impact of GPS navigation services on the number of heavy goods vehicles travelling through villages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is no legal requirement for drivers of any vehicle to have or use satellite navigation equipment and the Government currently has no plans to mandate their use. If such equipment is provided, it is important that it is suitable for the vehicle being...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Outdoor Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has made an assessment of the merits of outdoor education for (a) children and (b) adults.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department believes that outdoor education can be a valuable part of a broad and balanced curriculum, for example in physical education and geography, and a school’s enrichment offer. Both form an important part of our mission to break down barriers t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Chronic Illnesses: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that diagnostic results shared via the NHS App for patients living with chronic conditions are accompanied by (a) tailored information for patients abo
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the delivery of the 10-Year Health Plan, the NHS App will provide an improved and personalised experience for users, empowering them to access key elements of their health conditions like test results, and providing patients with 24 hour a day,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Mayors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department plans to take to ensure that new Mayors are accountable to their local areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Within Mayoral Combined Authorities and Mayoral Combined County Authorities there is an existing system of accountability and scrutiny arrangements. This system ensures that public spending is achieving value for money. In the English Devolution White Pap...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Project Gigabit: Ossett and Denby Dale
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what progress his Department has made on the rollout of Project Gigabit in Ossett and Denby Dale constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: According to the independent website Thinkbroadband.com, over 99% of premises in the Ossett and Denby Dale constituency can access superfast broadband speeds (>=30 Mbps) and over 96% have access to a gigabit-capable broadband connection (>1000 Mbps)...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport: Ossett and Denby Dale
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help ensure that the transport system supports economic growth in Ossett and Denby Dale constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Kickstarting economic growth across every corner of the UK is the top priority of this Government. We’re getting on with delivering the Government’s Plan for Change by delivering the basics of a better transport system, including in places across West Yo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Seasonal Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps he is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help farmers recruit people on Seasonal Worker visas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We know how important securing the right workforce is to the agri-food chain, for both skilled (such as butchers and vets) and temporary (such as seasonal horticulture harvesting) jobs. The Government has confirmed the Seasonal Worker visa route for 202...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: West Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent steps he has taken to help support farming businesses in West Yorkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government wants to improve profitability for farmers, including for those in West Yorkshire, to make their businesses viable for the future. That is why we were pleased to announce £5 billion for the farming budget over 2024/25 and 2025/26. The farm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Kidney Diseases: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help (a) prevent and (b) increase the early detection of chronic kidney disease through the 10-year health plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 10-Year Health Plan will deliver the three big shifts the National Health Service needs to be fit for the future, namely from hospital to community, from analogue to digital, and from sickness to prevention. All of these are relevant to improving the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help tackle fraud in the welfare system by serious and organised criminal gangs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DWP works collaboratively across Government Departments and Law Enforcement Agencies on investigations into benefit fraud carried out by organised crime gangs. New powers in the Public Authorities (Fraud, Error and Recovery) Bill will strengthen our capab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Penistone Line: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what progress her Department has made on upgrading the Penistone rail line.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Penistone Line is a Levelling Up Fund programme scheme valued at £47.9m and is subject to business case approval. Funding for Levelling Up Fund schemes is currently only confirmed until the end of financial year 2025/26. Funding for local transport ca...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Shepley Station: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has plans to provide disabled access at Shepley train station in Ossett and Denby Dale constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are carefully considering the best approach to the Access for All programme. Since April 2024, 19 stations have been completed with a further 8 due to be completed by April 2025. We will shortly be updating MPs and other stakeholders on our approach to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Endometriosis: West Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of the provision of endometriosis care services in West Yorkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to prioritising women’s health and improving the care received by those suffering from gynaecological conditions, including endometriosis. We have taken urgent action to tackle gynaecology waiting lists through the Elective Ref...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services: West Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many and what proportion of dentists are taking on new NHS patients in (a) Ossett and Denby Dale constituency and (b) West Yorkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 4 February 2025, there were 13 National Health Service dental practices in Ossett and Denby Dale constituency, with three, or 23%, showing as ‘accepting new child patients when availability allows’ and two, or 15%, showing as ‘accepting new adult pa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: West Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help reduce NHS waiting times in West Yorkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling waiting lists is a key part of our Health Mission and a top priority for this government. On 6 January 2025, NHS England published the new Elective Reform Plan, which sets out how we will cut the waiting list and get back to the 18-week constitut...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Arts: Economic Growth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment her Department has made of the contribution of the creative industries to economic growth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The creative industries sector is one of eight growth-driving sectors identified in the government’s Industrial Strategy. We will publish a Creative Industries Sector Plan to drive growth in the late spring.Creative industries GVA grew at 1.5 times the ra...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Yorkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what financial support she is providing to local transport authorities and Mayors to improve local bus services in Yorkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to delivering the better, more reliable bus services that passengers deserve. In the Budget the government confirmed investment of over £1 billion to support and improve bus services and keep fares affordable. Local transport a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Mineworkers' Pension Scheme: Ossett and Denby Dale
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of changes to the Mineworkers Pension Scheme on the living standards of the recipients of that pension in Ossett and Denby Dale constituenc
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the end of November last year, the first uprated payments for the Mineworkers Pension Scheme landed for over 100,000 miners and their families. This is an average increase of 32% on their monthly payments (or an average additional £28/week).
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
